passage:
Regular physical exercise offers a wide range of benefits for both the body and the mind. Studies consistently show that people who exercise at least three times a week have a significantly lower risk of developing heart disease, type 2 diabetes, and certain types of cancer. Exercise also strengthens muscles and bones, which becomes especially important as people age and face the risk of osteoporosis.

Beyond physical health, exercise has a profound effect on mental well-being. Physical activity triggers the release of endorphins — chemicals in the brain that act as natural mood elevators. Research has shown that regular exercise can be as effective as medication in treating mild to moderate depression. Furthermore, exercise improves sleep quality, reduces anxiety, and boosts self-confidence.

Despite these well-documented benefits, many people struggle to maintain a regular exercise routine. Common barriers include lack of time, motivation, and access to facilities. Experts suggest that even short bursts of activity — such as a 10-minute brisk walk — can contribute meaningfully to overall health when done consistently.

questions.question: Exercising three times a week reduces the risk of heart disease.
Exercise has no impact on mental health conditions.
Endorphins released during exercise can improve a person's mood.
Most people who fail to exercise regularly do so because they dislike physical activity.
Short bursts of activity can be beneficial if done consistently.
Exercise is more effective than all medications for treating depression.
